what_it_does: Defines CSS rules for the Vi assistant UI: a shared learned-feedback badge, animated thinking dots, a 'what I know' knowledge strip, a 'makeables' creation grid, an in-panel stream runner overlay, a memory-launcher foot row, and an apply-action message button.
observations: Heavily relies on CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, radii, typography, and motion.
Uses a warm gold/bronze accent palette throughout badges, pills, and interactive highlights.
Class names follow a BEM-like pattern with block, element, and modifier segments.
The 'makeables' section uses a two-column grid with thumbnail-like visual placeholders.
The stream runner is an absolute inset overlay inside the panel with its own scrollable body.
Interactive elements include hover transitions and cursor:pointer affordances.
No media queries or responsive breakpoints are present in this file.
Comments reference <ViLearned> and <ViThread> but selectors are class-based, not tag-based.
standouts: The shared 'shaped by your feedback' learned badge with mark and count pill.
The 'what I know' strip that surfaces context and provides a toggleable list.
The 'makeables' grid presenting actionable creation options inside the assistant panel.
The absolute-positioned in-panel stream runner overlay for streaming output.
conventions: BEM-style class naming with double underscores and --modifiers.
Extensive use of design-token CSS variables rather than hard-coded values.
Section comments that map rules to named UI areas.
Consistent pill/chip styling for tags, badges, and counts.
Flexbox and grid layouts with compact spacing.
concerns: Many custom properties are referenced without fallbacks, so missing tokens will break rendering.
The stream overlay uses z-index: 4 without any local stacking context explanation.
Class names are tightly coupled to the 'Vi' product concept, limiting generic reuse.
No responsive scaling or accessibility-focused rules like focus states are visible here.
The relationship between these class names and actual component markup is not shown.
notes: This file is the visual skin for Vi's feedback-aware badge, knowledge strip, creation grid, and streaming overlay within the floating panel.
questions: What exact markup elements map to classes like .vi-learned and .vi-knows?
What are the concrete values of the referenced CSS custom properties?
How is the in-panel stream runner shown, hidden, and populated?
What does 'token-driven only — see CLAUDE.md' specify in practice?
What are 'MS_VI_CHAT actions' and how do they relate to .vi-msg__apply?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose: it styles the described Vi badge, knowledge strip, makeables, and stream runner components.
intention_signals: It appears to build a cohesive, branded UI layer for an AI assistant panel that surfaces what it has learned, what it can create, and live streaming output, all within a compact floating interface.
novelty: The combination of a learned-feedback badge with count, a contextual 'what I know' strip, and a 'makeables' creation grid as first-class UI patterns for an assistant panel.
contribution: A reusable set of CSS component styles for an assistant panel's knowledge, creation, and streaming UI, expressed through a gold-accented custom-property design system.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS stylesheet defining the visual styles for the Vi assistant's learned-feedback badge, knowledge strip, makeables grid, in-panel stream runner overlay, and action button, using a gold-accented custom-property design system.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a shared, token-driven styling layer for Vi's context-aware floating panel and related assistant components.
